reconstruction_note: The blog post was published 2026-07-11; its original working fixtures (tmp/firsthand-*) were discarded per the finite-disk policy. The fixtures here were reconstructed from the post's recorded firsthand log and then RE-EXECUTED fresh on 2026-07-15 against the same pinned weaver 0.24.2. Because check/diff/generate are deterministic for a pinned version (no live DB, no timing), the re-run reproduces the original results exactly. evidence.txt is that fresh run's captured output — not a copied number.

environment.note: This verifies CLI behavior + exit codes on a fixed registry, not timing. CPU/GPU/RAM do not affect the result and are intentionally omitted. Exit codes are deterministic for weaver 0.24.2.

subject.name: weaver
subject.install: GitHub release v0.24.2 (weaver-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip). run.sh downloads the matching per-OS asset.
subject.note: Pinned. `latest` is intentionally NOT used: weaver's diagnostics and defaults evolve across releases, so an unpinned re-run would not be evidence of THIS behavior.

cited_not_measured.registry_dependency_depth: The a->b->c dependency-chain depth-10 support is cited from release notes, not exercised here.
cited_not_measured.live_check_github_action: weaver-live-check GH Action (0.24.0) is cited, not run.

discrepancy_flag.resolve_command: The published post says `weaver registry resolve` is deprecated and '안내하며 멈춘다' (guides you to generate/package and STOPS). Measured on 0.24.2: resolve prints the deprecation warning to stderr but THEN COMPLETES the resolution and exits 0 (see evidence.txt: resolve_deprecated_but_runs exit=0). It warns; it does not halt. Recorded here accurately; the post phrasing overstates it. Flagged for optional human correction of the post body.

raw_artifacts.retention_policy: no large artifacts; committed evidence is small and deterministic
raw_artifacts.note: The weaver binary, the downloaded zip, gen_out/, and .weaver/ are reproduction byproducts and are NOT committed. Committed evidence: the fixtures (reg*/, policies/, templates/), evidence.txt (exit-code matrix), diff.json (attribute-change output), and gen_out/attributes.md (generate output sample).
